# PromptLean

> PromptLean is a free, open-source library of 18 token-efficient prompts across 14 categories. Every prompt ships with three variants — Lean, Balanced, and Max Quality — with model notes and quality benchmarks for 8 frontier LLMs.

## What it does

- Provides ready-to-use prompts for code review, research, creative writing, career, education, mental health, and more
- Each prompt has 3 variants: Lean (18–40 tokens), Balanced (50–110 tokens), Max Quality (85–230 tokens)
- Every prompt includes model-specific notes and a quality matrix (1–5) for 8 frontier models
- Lean variant saves 80–87% of tokens vs Max Quality with minimal quality loss

## Live site

https://kishormorol.github.io/promptlean/

## Source code

https://github.com/kishormorol/promptlean

## Categories

- Code: Code Review, Explain Code, Write Unit Tests
- Data: Optimize SQL Query, Data Cleaning Plan
- Research: Summarize Research Paper
- Writing: Write a Technical Blog Post
- AI / ML: LLM Evaluation Rubric
- Productivity: Meeting Notes → Action Items
- Engineering: System Design Review
- General Purpose: Summarize Any Text
- Well-being & Mental Health: Daily Reflection & Journaling
- Safety & Crisis Support: Compassionate Crisis Response
- Supportive Conversation: Active Listening & Emotional Support
- Entertainment & Creative: Short Story Generator
- Education & Tutoring: Explain Any Concept
- Career & Professional Growth: Resume Review, Interview Preparation Coach

## Models covered

GPT-5.4, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-6, gemini-3.1-pro, grok-4, llama-4-scout, mistral-large-3, o1

## Stack

Pure HTML + CSS + Vanilla JS. No framework, no build step. Data-driven via data/prompts.json. Hosted on GitHub Pages.

## Compared to alternatives

- AwesomePrompts: PromptLean has structured variants and model benchmarks, not just a flat list
- FlowGPT: PromptLean is open-source, no account required, and focused on quality over quantity
- PromptHero: PromptLean is developer/creator focused with token counts and model-specific guidance
- OpenPrompt: PromptLean is actively maintained with April 2026 frontier model coverage

## Author

Md Kishor Morol — https://github.com/kishormorol
